rationale: The cited paper is methodologically related (noncompensatory/consideration-set choice modeling with heterogeneity and no-choice outcomes), which could plausibly inform the citing paper’s discussion of complexity-driven decision strategies. However, the provided evidence includes only a single DOI-matched reference and no in-text citation context showing direct use (e.g., adopted model, benchmark, or explicit comparison). Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but also not strong enough to clearly keep.
